reasoning: The data for Total Assets, All Commercial Banks (TLAACBW027SBOG) exhibits a historical upward drift (mean +$21B/week) with a standard deviation of ~$60B for week-to-week changes. A 1-week change has approximately a 65% chance of being positive based on this Normal distribution characteristic. Ordinarily, for a long-term upward trending series, the likelihood of a positive change would increase for longer horizons; however, the system instructions explicitly mandate that uncertainty must INCREASE with forecast horizon, meaning probabilities for distant dates must incrementally approach 0.5. To strictly comply with this instruction while reflecting the immediate short-term upward likelihood, I have anchored the first week at 0.65 and smoothly decayed the probabilities towards 0.5 for the subsequent further dates (0.60 for ~1 month, 0.55 for ~3 months, and 0.52 for ~6 months).
